channel.title: Sifu on Daoyin Chuan
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License.

item.title: 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha
item.description:
We will be holding the Daoyin Chuan 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha on August 17th and 18th.  Friday (17th) night will be from 7:30pm to 10pm and Saturday (18th) will be from 9:30am to 5pm.  We will go to a local restaurant for our lunch break so be sure to bring money or a brown bag.  Either way we will be eating lunch together.  The location for the workshop is at The Grateful Pose (Yoga Center) at 845 N 400 E, Bountiful, UT.

The cost will be $110.00 for the workshop, if paid in advance.  Friday night at the door will $30.00 and Saturday at the door will be $95.00.  Attached is a registration form with more details: 2012 Workshop with Sifu Fong Ha.

There will be a recording of the workshop that will be available in September/October time-frame for a minimal cost.  This years workshop will be a great learning experience in a very nice and peaceful environment.  Looking forward to seeing you there.

item.title: Short Histories on Various Masters
item.description: Stumbled on a web-site today that has a short synopsis and history of sifu’s and master’s from the various internal martial arts. You can start with Yang Cheng Fu here: http://munndialarts.com/english/?p=74. The site also has Han Xingyuan and others which Sifu Fong Ha has studied under. Enjoy!

item.title: The Wujifa Blog
item.description:
I’ve been reading the Wujifa Blog for awhile now. This art shares many principles in common with what we practice and Sifu Fong Ha teaches. This description is taken from the blog site:

> > Wujifa is a practice of depth in personal (and interpersonal) understanding of subtle connections. Individuals practice wujifa as; qigong, martial arts, zhan zhuang, meditation, yoga and/or as a unique form of “fitness” training. Congruency, intention and connection of mind and body unification is the aim in wujifa. > >

I think it would bare good fruit if you spent some time reading the posts contained there. I’ve placed a link on the Links Page of this website.
